name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّكُمْ ۖ لَا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنۢ بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلَا يَخْرُجْنَ إِلَّآ أَن يَأْتِينَ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ ۚ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُۥ ۚ لَا تَدْرِى لَعَلَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرًا (١)
```

```english
O Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them at [the conclusion of] their term and calculate the term, and be wary of Allah, your Lord. Do not turn them out from their homes, nor shall they go out, unless they commit a gross indecency. These are Allah’s bounds, and whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah certainly wrongs himself. You never know maybe Allah will bring off something new later on. (1)
```

```arabic
فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ وَأَشْهِدُوا۟ ذَوَىْ عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِۦ مَن كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مَخْرَجًا (٢)
```

```english
Then, when they have completed their term, either retain them honourably or separate from them honourably, and take the witness of two honest men from among yourselves, and bear witness for the sake of Allah. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day is advised to [comply with] this. Whoever is wary of Allah, He shall make for him a way out [of the adversities of the world and the Hereafter] (2)
```

```arabic
وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَـٰلِغُ أَمْرِهِۦ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدْرًا (٣)
```

```english
and provide for him from whence he does not count upon. And whoever puts his trust in Allah, He will suffice him. Indeed Allah carries through His commands. Certainly, Allah has ordained a measure [and extent] for everything. (3)
```

```arabic
وَٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى يَئِسْنَ مِنَ ٱلْمَحِيضِ مِن نِّسَآئِكُمْ إِنِ ٱرْتَبْتُمْ فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلَـٰثَةُ أَشْهُرٍ وَٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى لَمْ يَحِضْنَ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰتُ ٱلْأَحْمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَن يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِهِۦ يُسْرًا (٤)
```

```english
As for those of your wives who have ceased having menses—[or] if you have any doubts [concerning its cause, whether it’s is age or something else]—their term [of waiting] and of those who have not yet had menses, shall be three months. As for those who are pregnant, their term shall be until they deliver. And whoever is wary of Allah, He shall grant him ease in his affairs. (4)
```

```arabic
ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ أَنزَلَهُۥٓ إِلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِۦ وَيُعْظِمْ لَهُۥٓ أَجْرًا (٥)
```

```english
That is Allah’s ordinance, which He has sent down to you, and whoever is wary of Allah, He shall absolve him of his misdeeds and give him a great reward. (5)
```

```arabic
أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ سَكَنتُم مِّن وُجْدِكُمْ وَلَا تُضَآرُّوهُنَّ لِتُضَيِّقُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ ۚ وَإِن كُنَّ أُو۟لَـٰتِ حَمْلٍ فَأَنفِقُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ فَإِنْ أَرْضَعْنَ لَكُمْ فَـَٔاتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ ۖ وَأْتَمِرُوا۟ بَيْنَكُم بِمَعْرُوفٍ ۖ وَإِن تَعَاسَرْتُمْ فَسَتُرْضِعُ لَهُۥٓ أُخْرَىٰ (٦)
```

```english
House them where you live, in accordance with your means, and do not harass them to put them in straits, and should they be pregnant, maintain them until they deliver. Then, if they suckle [the baby] for you, give them their wages and consult together honourably. But if you make things difficult for each other, then another woman will suckle [the baby] for him. (6)
```

```arabic
لِيُنفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِّن سَعَتِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُۥ فَلْيُنفِقْ مِمَّآ ءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ لَا يُكَلِّفُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا مَآ ءَاتَىٰهَا ۚ سَيَجْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ بَعْدَ عُسْرٍ يُسْرًا (٧)
```

```english
Let the affluent man spend out of his affluence, and let he whose provision has been tightened spend out of what Allah has given him. Allah does not task any soul except [according to] what He has given it. Allah will bring about ease after hardship. (7)
```

## (Commentary)

The Surah contains an exposition of the general principles regarding the rulings of divorce (الطلاق), followed by exhortation, warning, and glad tidings. The Surah is Medinan, as evidenced by its context (السياق).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ** — "O Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them at [the onset of] their waiting period, and keep the count of the waiting period" (65:1), until the end of the verse. The discourse begins by addressing the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) because he is the Messenger to the community (الأمة) and their leader (إمام); thus, it is appropriate for the address to him to encompass both him and his followers from his community. This is common in usage, where the leader and master of a people is singled out in the call (النداء), yet the address pertains to what concerns both him and his people. Therefore, there is no need for the claim of some that the implied meaning is: "O Prophet, say to your community: When you divorce women..."

His saying: **إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ** — "When you divorce women, divorce them at [the onset of] their waiting period" (65:1) means: when you intend to divorce women and are on the verge of doing so. This is because there is no meaning to effecting divorce after the divorce has already occurred. It is similar to His saying: **إِذا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا** — "When you stand up for prayer, wash..." (5:6).

The **waiting period** (العدة) — *the period during which a woman refrains from remarrying after divorce or the death of her husband until the legally prescribed duration expires* — refers to the woman's abstention from a husband until the duration established by the Sharia (الشرع) concludes. The meaning of "divorcing them for their waiting period" is to divorce them at the time of their waiting period such that the duration of the waiting period begins from the day the divorce is effected. This occurs by the divorce taking place during a period of purity (طهر) in which no sexual intercourse has occurred, until her menstrual cycles (أقراؤها) are completed.

His saying: **وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ** — "and keep the count of the waiting period" (65:1) means: count the cycles (الأقراء) by which she observes the waiting period. This is to safeguard it, because during this time, the woman has the right to maintenance (النفقة) and housing (السكنى) from her husband, and the husband has the right of return (الرجوع).

His saying: **وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ لا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنْ بُيُوتِهِنَّ** — "and be God-conscious of Allah, your Lord. Do not turn them out of their homes" (65:1). The apparent context suggests that **لا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ** (do not turn them out) is an appositive (بدل) for **اتَّقُوا اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ** (be God-conscious of Allah, your Lord), which serves to emphasize the prohibition in "do not turn them out." What is meant by "their homes" are the houses they were residing in before the divorce; they are attributed to the women in consideration of their residency (السكنى).

His saying: **وَلا يَخْرُجْنَ** — "nor shall they leave" (65:1) is a prohibition against them leaving of their own accord, just as the preceding phrase was a prohibition against them being turned out.

His saying: **إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ** — "unless they commit a manifest indecency" (65:1) means: an evident one, such as adultery (الزنا), foul language (البذاء), or harming the husband's family, as mentioned in the narrations transmitted from the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام).

His saying: **وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ** — "These are the bounds of Allah, and whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah certainly wrongs himself" (65:1). That is, the aforementioned rulings of divorce are the bounds of Allah (حدود الله) — *the legal limits and ordinances set by Allah* — by which He has delimited your actions. Whoever transgresses and exceeds the bounds of Allah by not observing them and by opposing them has wronged himself, meaning he has disobeyed his Lord.

His saying: **لا تَدْرِي لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً** — "You do not know, maybe Allah will bring about some new matter after that" (65:1). That is, a matter that necessitates a change in the situation and a shift in the husband's opinion regarding her divorce, such that he inclines toward reconciliation (الالتيام) and there appears in his heart a love for returning to the previous state.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَإِذا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ** — "Then, when they have completed their term, either retain them honorably or separate from them honorably" (65:2), until His saying: **وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ** — "and the Last Day" (65:2). The meaning of "reaching their term" is their approaching the end of the waiting period and being on the verge of it. "Retaining them" (إمساكهن) is used metaphorically for taking them back (الرجوع), and "separating from them" (مفارقتهن) means leaving them so that they complete the waiting period and become irrevocably divorced (يبن).

The meaning of the retention being "honorably" (بمعروف) is good companionship and observing the rights Allah has established for them. The separation being "honorably" also means the fulfillment of legal rights; thus, the implied meaning is "honorably according to the Sharia."

His saying: **وَأَشْهِدُوا ذَوَيْ عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ** — "and take as witness two honest men from among yourselves" (65:2) means: take as witnesses to the divorce two men from among you who possess justice (عدل). The clarification of the meaning of justice (العدل) — *the quality of moral integrity and adherence to religious law* — has already been provided in the commentary (تفسير) of Surah al-Baqarah.

His saying: **وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهادَةَ لِلَّهِ** — "and bear witness for the sake of Allah" (65:2). Its clarification was also provided in the commentary of Surah al-Baqarah.

His saying: **ذلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَنْ كانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ** — "This is what he who believes in Allah and the Last Day is advised [to do]" (65:2). That is, the preceding command to be God-conscious of Allah, to bear witness for Allah, and the prohibition against transgressing the bounds of Allah—or the totality of the preceding rulings, the exhortation toward godwariness (التقوى) and sincerity in testimony, and the deterrent against transgressing the bounds of Allah—is an advice given to the believers so they may rely upon the truth and uproot themselves from falsehood. This implies that turning away from these rulings or altering them constitutes a departure from faith (الإيمان).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ** — "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will maintain a way out for him, and provide for him from whence he does not reckon" (65:2-3), until His saying: **قَدْراً** — "a measure" (65:3). That is, "whoever is God-conscious of Allah" and refrains from His prohibitions, does not transgress His bounds, and respects His laws by acting upon them, "He will maintain a way out for him" from the straits of life's problems. For His Sharia is primordial (فطرية), through which Allah guides man to what his innate nature (فطرة) requires, what his needs dictate, and what guarantees his happiness in this world and the Hereafter. "And provide for him" (وَيَرْزُقْهُ) — in terms of a spouse, wealth, and everything he requires for a wholesome life and a pure existence — "from whence he does not reckon" (مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ) and does not expect. Thus, the believer should not fear that if he is God-conscious of Allah and respects His bounds, he will be deprived of the goodness of life or afflicted with a constricted living, for provision (الرزق) is guaranteed, and Allah is powerful over what He has guaranteed.

**وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ** — "And whoever puts his trust in Allah" (65:3) by detaching from his own self regarding what it desires and commands, and by preferring the will of Allah, the Glorified, over his own will, and the action Allah desires over the action his self desires and wants—in other words, by following the religion (دين) of Allah and acting according to His rulings—**فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ** — "He will suffice him" (65:3). That is, He is sufficient for him in what he desires of a wholesome life and what he hopes for of happiness through his primordial nature (فطرة), not through his false imagination (واهمته).

This is because He, the Exalted, is the Supreme Cause (السبب الأعلى) to which all causes lead. When He wills a thing, He does it and achieves what He willed without His will changing—for He is the one who says: **ما يُبَدَّلُ الْقَوْلُ لَدَيَّ** — "The word is not changed with Me" (50:29)—and without any barrier coming between Him and what He willed—for He is the one who says: **وَاللهُ يَحْكُمُ لا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِ** — "Allah judges, and there is none to reverse His judgment" (13:41). As for the other causes (الأسباب) that man clings to for the fulfillment of his needs, they only possess as much causality (السببية) as Allah, the Glorified, has granted them. He is the Owner of what He has granted them and the Powerful over what He has enabled them to do; they possess only as much action as Allah has permitted.

Thus, Allah is sufficient for the one who trusts in Him, and none other. **إِنَّ اللهَ بالِغُ أَمْرِهِ** — "Indeed Allah carries through His command" (65:3); He reaches wherever He wills. He is the one who says: **إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ** — "All His command, when He wills something, is to say to it ‘Be,’ and it is" (36:82). **قَدْ جَعَلَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْراً** — "Allah has certainly set a measure for everything" (65:3). There is nothing except that it has a determined measure (قدر مقدور) and a delimited bound (حد محدود), while Allah, the Glorified, is not limited by any bound, nor does anything encompass Him, for He encompasses all things.

This is the meaning of the verse considering its occurrence within the context of the verses of divorce and its application to that specific case.

However, considering its absolute sense (إطلاقها) in itself, regardless of the context in which it occurred, His saying: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ** signifies that whoever is God-conscious of Allah according to the true meaning of godwariness (تقوى)—and this is not achieved except by knowing Him, the Exalted, through His names and attributes, then refraining and being God-conscious by avoiding prohibitions and guarding against neglecting obligations purely for His Noble Face—the necessary consequence of this is that he desires nothing except what Allah desires of action or omission. The consequence of this is that his will is consumed within the will of Allah, such that no action proceeds from him except by a will from Allah.

The consequence of that is that he sees himself and what pertains to him of characteristics or actions as the absolute property of Allah, the Glorified, who disposes of them as He wills. This is the guardianship (ولاية) of Allah — *His divine authority and protection over His servants* — where He takes charge of His servant's affairs, such that nothing of ownership in its true sense remains for the servant except what Allah, the Glorified, has granted him; and He is the Owner of what He has granted, and all sovereignty (الملك) belongs to Allah, exalted is His Name (عز إسمه).

At that point, Allah saves him from the straits of illusion (الوهم) and the prison of polytheism (الشرك) — *the act of associating partners with Allah* — caused by attachment to apparent causes, and "He will maintain a way out for him, and provide for him from whence he does not reckon." As for material provision, he used to see it as the result of his own efforts and the apparent causes he relied upon, while he knew only a little of the many causes—like a spark of fire that illuminates for a person in a dark night only the place of his foot, while he is heedless of what lies beyond it. But Allah, the Glorified, encompasses all causes; He is their Arranger, arranging them as He wills and permitting the influence of those hidden causes of which man has no knowledge.

As for spiritual provision (الرزق المعنوي), which is the true provision by which the human soul lives and endures, it is something he did not reckon upon, nor did he reckon the path of its arrival to him.

In short, He, the Glorified, takes charge of his affair, brings him out from the pit of destruction, and provides for him from whence he does not reckon. He loses nothing of his perfection or the blessings he hoped to attain through his efforts, because he put his trust in Allah and delegated to his Lord what belonged to himself. **وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ** — "And whoever puts his trust in Allah, He will suffice him," rather than all other apparent causes that sometimes fail and sometimes succeed. **إِنَّ اللهَ بالِغُ أَمْرِهِ** — "Indeed Allah carries through His command," because all matters are delimited and encompassed by Him, the Exalted, and **قَدْ جَعَلَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْراً** — "Allah has certainly set a measure for everything," so nothing is outside the measure He has determined for it.

This is the portion of the righteous (الصالحين) among the friends (الأولياء) of Allah from this verse.

As for those below them among the average believers (المؤمنين المتوسطين) who possess godwariness, whose degrees are lower in terms of knowledge and action, they have a share of Allah's guardianship (ولاية) that suits their state in the sincerity of faith and righteous deeds (العمل الصالح). He, the Exalted, has said absolutely: **وَاللهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ** — "and Allah is the guardian of the faithful" (3:68), and He said absolutely: **وَاللهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ** — "and Allah is the guardian of the God-conscious" (45:19).

Their adherence to the religion of truth—which is the way of life—and their proceeding in all matters based on His will, the Exalted, constitutes godwariness (تقوى) of Allah and trust (توكل) in Him by placing His will in the place of their own wills. Thus, they attain happiness in life accordingly; Allah makes a way out for them and provides for them from whence they do not reckon. Their Lord is sufficient for them, for He carries through His command and has set a measure for everything.

They suffer a deprivation of happiness in proportion to the polytheism (الشرك) that creeps into their faith and actions. He, the Exalted, has said: **وَما يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللهِ إِلَّا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ** — "And most of them do not believe in Allah without ascribing partners [to Him]" (12:106). He also said absolutely: **إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ** — "Indeed Allah does not forgive that any partner should be ascribed to Him" (4:48).

And He said: **وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٌ لِمَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً** — "Indeed I am most forgiving toward him who repents, believes, and acts righteously" (20:82), meaning for the one who repents from polytheism. He also said absolutely: **وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ** — "and plead to Allah for forgiveness; indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful" (73:20).

Thus, a believer does not ascend to a degree among the degrees of Allah's guardianship (ولاية) except by repenting from the hidden polytheism (الشرك الخفي) that lies below it.

This verse is among the most brilliant of Quranic verses. The commentators have scattered words regarding its phrases which we have set aside.

His saying, the Exalted: **وَاللَّائِي يَئِسْنَ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلاثَةُ أَشْهُرٍ** — "As for those of your women who have passed the age of menstruation, if you are in doubt, their waiting period shall be three months" (65:4). What is meant by "doubt" (الارتياب) is uncertainty regarding their cessation of menstruation: is it due to old age or some incidental cause? So the meaning is: as for those of your women who have ceased menstruating and you are in doubt whether their cessation is due to reaching the age of menopause (سن اليأس) or due to an incidental cause, their waiting period is three months.

His saying: **وَاللَّائِي لَمْ يَحِضْنَ** — "and [also] those who have not yet menstruated" (65:4) is conjoined to His saying: "As for those who have passed the age..." etc. The meaning is: and those who have not yet menstruated, though they are at an age where women typically menstruate, their waiting period is three months.

His saying: **وَأُولاتُ الْأَحْمالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ** — "As for those who are pregnant, their term shall be until they deliver their burden" (65:4). That is, the end of their waiting period is the delivery of the child.

His saying: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ يُسْراً** — "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will make his matter easy for him" (65:4). That is, He will ease for him the hardships and difficulties he faces. It is also said: it means He eases for him the affairs of this world and the Hereafter, either through immediate relief or delayed compensation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ذلِكَ أَمْرُ اللهِ أَنْزَلَهُ إِلَيْكُمْ** — "That is Allah’s ordinance, which He has sent down to you" (65:5). That is, what has been explained in the preceding verses is the ruling of Allah which He sent down to you. In His saying: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّئاتِهِ وَيُعْظِمْ لَهُ أَجْراً** — "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will absolve him of his misdeeds and maximize his reward" (65:5), there is an indication that following the commands is part of godwariness (التقوى), just like avoiding prohibitions. This is perhaps because complying with a command necessitates avoiding its neglect.

The "absolution of misdeeds" (تكفير السيئات) is their concealment through forgiveness. What is meant by "misdeeds" (السيئات) are the minor sins (المعاصي الصغيرة); thus, godwariness remains concerned with the major sins (كبائر المعاصي). The totality of His saying: "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will absolve him of his misdeeds and maximize his reward" carries the same meaning as His saying: **إِنْ تَجْتَنِبُوا كَبائِرَ ما تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُكَفِّرْ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ وَنُدْخِلْكُمْ مُدْخَلاً كَرِيماً** — "If you avoid the major sins that you are forbidden, We will absolve you of your misdeeds and admit you to a noble abode" (4:31). From these two verses, it appears that what is meant by "prohibitions" (المحارم) in the narration of the Imam (عليه‌السلام) defining godwariness as "refraining from the prohibitions of Allah" refers to the major sins.

It also appears that opposing what Allah has sent down regarding the command of divorce and the waiting period is among the major sins, since the godwariness mentioned in the verse necessarily includes what was mentioned regarding the matter of divorce and the waiting period; thus, it is not among the "misdeeds" that are absolved, otherwise the meaning of the verse would be undermined.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ سَكَنْتُمْ مِنْ وُجْدِكُمْ** — "House them where you live, in accordance with your means" (65:6), until the end of the verse. It is stated in *al-Mufradat*: "His saying, the Exalted: **مِنْ وُجْدِكُمْ** means your capacity and the extent of your wealth. Wealth is expressed as *wujdan* and *jidah*. It has been reported with all three vowel markings on the *waw* (*wajd*, *wujd*, *wijd*)." [End of quote].

The pronoun in **أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ** (house them) refers to the divorced women, as supported by the context. The meaning is: house the divorced women where you reside, in dwellings according to your capacity and wealth—the wealthy according to his means and the poor according to his.

His saying: **وَلا تُضآرُّوهُنَّ لِتُضَيِّقُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ** — "and do not distress them in order to straiten them" (65:6). That is, do not direct any harm toward them that is difficult for them to bear in terms of housing, clothing, and maintenance, so as to bring about distress and hardship upon them.

His saying: **وَإِنْ كُنَّ أُولاتِ حَمْلٍ فَأَنْفِقُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ حَتَّى يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ** — "And if they are pregnant, maintain them until they deliver their burden" (65:6). Its meaning is clear.

His saying: **فإِنْ أَرْضَعْنَ لَكُمْ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ** — "Then, if they suckle [the baby] for you, give them their wages" (65:6). They are entitled to the wage for breastfeeding from you, which is part of the child's maintenance that falls upon the father.

His saying: **وَأْتَمِرُوا بَيْنَكُمْ بِمَعْرُوفٍ** — "and consult among yourselves honorably" (65:6). **Consultation** (الائتمار) — *the act of mutual deliberation and reaching an agreement* — regarding a matter is when a group deliberates such that they command one another within it. This is an address to both the man and the woman, meaning: consult one another regarding the child's affair and agree upon what is honorable (بمعروف) according to custom, such that the man is not harmed by an excessive wage he pays, nor the woman by a deficient one, nor the child by a reduction in the duration of breastfeeding, and so on.

His saying: **وَإِنْ تَعاسَرْتُمْ فَسَتُرْضِعُ لَهُ أُخْرى** — "but if you find it difficult for each other, then another woman shall suckle [the baby] for him" (65:6). That is, if each of you demands from the other what causes difficulty and you disagree, then another woman—a stranger other than his mother—shall breastfeed the child. That is, the father should seek a wet nurse other than the child's mother.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **لِيُنْفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِنْ سَعَتِهِ** — "Let the affluent man spend out of his affluence" (65:7). Spending from affluence means being generous in spending. It is a command to those of means to be generous toward their divorced wives who are breastfeeding their children.

His saying: **وَمَنْ قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنْفِقْ مِمَّا آتاهُ اللهُ** — "and he whose provision has been straitened shall spend out of what Allah has given him" (65:7). **Straitening** (قدر) of provision means its constriction, and **giving** (الإيتاء) means bestowing. The meaning is: he whose provision is constricted and is poor, unable to be generous in spending, should spend according to what Allah has given him of wealth; that is, he should spend according to his capacity.

His saying: **لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا ما آتاها** — "Allah does not task any soul except [according to] what He has given it" (65:7). That is, Allah does not burden any soul except to the extent of the capacity He has given it. This sentence negates hardship (الحرج) in divine obligations, including the maintenance of a divorced woman.

His saying: **سَيَجْعَلُ اللهُ بَعْدَ عُسْرٍ يُسْراً** — "Allah will bring about ease after hardship" (65:7). In this is glad tidings and consolation.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri who said: "Surah al-Nisa' al-Qusra (the shorter Surah of Women) was revealed seven years after the one in al-Baqarah."

I say: Surah al-Nisa' al-Qusra is Surah al-Talaq.

In the same source, Malik, al-Shafi'i, Abd al-Razzaq in *al-Musannaf*, Ahmad, Abd ibn Humayd, al-Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, Ibn Majah, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Abu Ya'la, Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Bayhaqi in his *Sunan* narrated from Ibn Umar that he divorced his wife while she was menstruating. This was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) became angry about it. Then he said: "Let him take her back, then keep her until she becomes pure, then menstruates, then becomes pure. Then, if it seems fit to him to divorce her, let him divorce her while she is pure before he touches her. That is the waiting period (العدة) for which Allah commanded that women be divorced." And the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) recited: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ** "in the beginning of their waiting period" (في قبل عدتهن).

**I say:** The phrase "in the beginning of their waiting period" (في قبل عدتهن) is the recitation of Ibn Umar, while what is in the codex (المصحف) is **لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ** (at [the onset of] their waiting period).

In the same source, Ibn al-Mundhir narrated from Ibn Sirin regarding His saying: **لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً** — "maybe Allah will bring about some new matter after that" (65:1). He said: "Regarding Hafsah bint Umar; the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) divorced her once, then there was revealed: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ** — 'O Prophet! When you divorce women...' until His saying: **يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً** — 'bring about some new matter after that.' He said: So he took her back."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Zurarah, from Abu Ja'far (عليه‌السلام) that he said: **كل طلاق لا يكون على السنة أو على العدة فليس بشيء** — "Every divorce that is not according to the Sunnah or according to the waiting period (العدة) is nothing." Zurarah said: I said to Abu Ja'far (عليه‌السلام): "Explain to me the divorce of the Sunnah (طلاق السنة) and the divorce of the waiting period (طلاق العدة)." He said: "As for the divorce of the Sunnah: when a man intends to divorce his wife, he waits for her until she menstruates and then becomes pure. When she emerges from her menstruation, he divorces her with one divorce without sexual intercourse, and takes two witnesses to that. Then he leaves her until she menstruates twice more, so her waiting period concludes with three menstruations, and she becomes irrevocably separated from him. He then becomes one of the suitors among others; if she wishes, she marries him, and if she wishes, she does not. He is responsible for her maintenance and housing as long as she is in her period, and they inherit from each other until the waiting period concludes."

He said: "As for the divorce of the waiting period (طلاق العدة) which Allah the Exalted mentioned: **فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ** — 'divorce them at [the onset of] their waiting period, and keep the count of the waiting period' (65:1): when one of you intends to divorce his wife the divorce of the waiting period, let him wait for her until she menstruates and emerges from her menstruation. Then he divorces her with one divorce without sexual intercourse and takes two just witnesses. He may take her back on that very day if he wishes, or days after that before she menstruates, and he takes witnesses to her return and has intercourse with her. She remains with him until she menstruates. When she menstruates and emerges from her menstruation, he divorces her with another divorce without sexual intercourse and takes witnesses to that. Then he takes her back again whenever he wishes before she menstruates, takes witnesses to her return, and has intercourse with her. She remains with him until she menstruates the third time. When she emerges from her third menstruation, he divorces her with the third divorce without sexual intercourse and takes witnesses to that. When he has done that, she is irrevocably separated from him and is not lawful for him until she marries a husband other than him."

It was said to him: "What if she is among those who do not menstruate?" He said: "Such a woman is divorced according to the divorce of the Sunnah."

In *Qurb al-Isnad*, with its chain of transmission from Safwan, who said: I heard—meaning Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام)—when a man came and asked him, saying: "I divorced my wife three times in one sitting." He replied: **ليس بشيء** — "It is nothing." Then he said: "Do you not read the Book of Allah the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ لا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنْ بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلا يَخْرُجْنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ** — 'O Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them at [the onset of] their waiting period, and keep the count of the waiting period, and be God-conscious of Allah, your Lord. Do not turn them out of their homes, nor shall they leave, unless they commit a manifest indecency' (65:1)."

Then he said: "Do you not know: **لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً** — 'maybe Allah will bring about some new matter after that' (65:1)?" Then he said: **كلما خالف كتاب الله والسنة ـ فهو يرد إلى كتاب الله والسنة** — "Whatever opposes the Book of Allah and the Sunnah is returned to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: regarding the meaning of His saying: **لا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنْ بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلا يَخْرُجْنَ ـ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ** — "Do not turn them out of their homes, nor shall they leave, unless they commit a manifest indecency" (65:1). He said: "It is not lawful for a man to turn out his wife when he has divorced her—and he still has the right of return over her—from his house, nor is it lawful for her to leave his house, unless they commit a manifest indecency."

The meaning of "indecency" (الفاحشة) is that she commits adultery or steals from the man. Also among the indecencies is being foul-mouthed (السلاطة) toward her husband. If she does any of that, it becomes lawful for him to turn her out.

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Wahb ibn Hafs, from one of the two [Imams] (عليهما‌السلام) regarding the divorced woman: "She observes the waiting period in her house and displays her adornment to him—maybe Allah will bring about some new matter after that."

**I say:** Regarding these meanings and the meanings of the phrases in the two verses, there are other narrations from the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام).

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Mu'awiyah ibn Wahb, from Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام), who said: **من أعطي ثلاثا لم يمنع ثلاثا : من أعطي الدعاء أعطي الإجابة ، ومن أعطي الشكر أعطي الزيادة ، ومن أعطي التوكل أعطي الكفاية** — "Whoever is given three things is not denied three things: whoever is given [the success to] supplicate is given the response; whoever is given [the success to] be grateful is given increase; and whoever is given [the success to] trust (التوكل) is given sufficiency (الكفاية)."

He said: "Have you not read the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic? **وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ** — 'And whoever puts his trust in Allah, He will suffice him' (65:3). And He said: **لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ** — 'If you are grateful, I will surely enhance you [in blessing]' (14:7). And He said: **ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ** — 'Call Me, I will answer you' (40:60)."

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: I asked Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام) about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ** — "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will maintain a way out for him, and provide for him from whence he does not reckon" (65:2-3). He said: **في دنياه** — "In his worldly life."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, who said: "This verse: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً** — 'And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will maintain a way out for him' (65:2) was revealed regarding a man from [the tribe of] Ashja' who was afflicted by hardship and calamity. The enemy had captured his son, so he came to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), who said: 'Be God-conscious of Allah and be patient.' Then his son, who was a captive, returned, for Allah had released him. He came to them having acquired some goats. He came and mentioned that to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and the verse was revealed. The Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: 'They are yours.'"

In the same source, Abu Ya'la, Abu Nu'aym, and al-Daylami narrated through the path of Ata ibn Yasar from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said regarding His saying: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً** — "And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will maintain a way out for him" (65:2): **من شبهات الدنيا ومن غمرات الموت ـ ومن شدائد يوم القيامة** — "From the uncertainties (شبهات) of the world, from the agonies of death, and from the hardships of the Day of Resurrection."

In the same source, al-Hakim (who authenticated it), Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Bayhaqi narrated from Abu Dharr, who said: "The Messenger of Allah began reciting this verse: **وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ** — 'And whoever is God-conscious of Allah, He will maintain a way out for him, and provide for him from whence he does not reckon' (65:2-3). He kept repeating it until I became drowsy. Then he said: 'O Abu Dharr, if all people were to take hold of it, it would suffice them.'"

In the same source, Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Tabarani, and al-Khatib narrated from Imran ibn Husayn, who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: **من انقطع إلى الله كفاه الله كل مئونة ـ ورزقه من حيث لا يحتسب ـ ومن انقطع إلى الدنيا وكله الله إليها** — "Whoever devotes himself entirely to Allah, Allah will suffice him in every burden and provide for him from whence he does not reckon. And whoever devotes himself entirely to the world, Allah entrusts him to it."

In the same source, Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from Ibn Abbas, attributing the tradition to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), who said: **من أحب أن يكون أقوى الناس فليتوكل على الله ، ومن أحب أن يكون أغنى الناس ـ فليكن بما في يد الله أوثق منه بما في يده ، ومن أحب أن يكون أكرم الناس فليتق الله** — "Whoever loves to be the strongest of people, let him put his trust in Allah. Whoever loves to be the wealthiest of people, let him be more confident in what is in Allah's hand than in what is in his own hand. And whoever loves to be the most noble of people, let him be God-conscious of Allah."

**I say:** The meaning of these narrations has already been provided in the conclusion of the discussion on the verses.

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام), who said: "The waiting period (العدة) for a woman who does not menstruate, and the woman with chronic bleeding (المستحاضة) who does not become pure, is three months. The waiting period for one who menstruates and whose menstruation is regular is three cycles (قروء)." I asked him about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ** — "if you are in doubt" (65:4). "What is the doubt (الريبة)?" He said: "Whatever exceeds a month is a doubt; so let her observe a waiting period of three months and leave the menstruation..." [the tradition continues].

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Muhammad ibn Qays, from Abu Ja'far (عليه‌السلام), who said: "The waiting period for a pregnant woman is that she delivers her burden, and she is entitled to maintenance honorably until she delivers her burden."

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani, from Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام), who said: "When a man divorces a woman while she is pregnant, he maintains her until she delivers her burden. When she has delivered it, he gives her her wage and does not distress her—unless he finds someone who has a cheaper wage than her. If she is satisfied with that wage, she has more right to her son until she weans him."

In *al-Faqih*, with its chain of transmission from Rib'i ibn Abdillah and al-Fudayl ibn Yasar, from Abu Abdillah (عليه‌السلام) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَمَنْ قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنْفِقْ مِمَّا آتاهُ اللهُ** — "and he whose provision has been straitened shall spend out of what Allah has given him" (65:7). He said: "If he spends on her what sustains her along with clothing [it is fine]; otherwise, they are to be separated."

**I say:** It was also narrated in *al-Kafi* with its chain of transmission from Abu Basir from him (عليه‌السلام).

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: regarding His saying: **وَأُولاتُ الْأَحْمالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ** — "As for those who are pregnant, their term shall be until they deliver their burden" (65:4). He said: "The term for a pregnant divorced woman is that she delivers what is in her womb. If she delivers on the day her husband divorces her, she may marry once she becomes pure. If she delivers what is in her womb up to nine months, she does not marry until she delivers."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj, from Abu al-Hasan (عليه‌السلام), who said: I asked him about a pregnant woman whose husband divorces her and she delivers a miscarriage—whether fully formed or not, or even if she delivers a lump of flesh (مضغة). He said: "Anything she delivers that is clearly a pregnancy, whether fully formed or not, her waiting period has concluded."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn al-Mundhir narrated from Mughirah, who said: I said to al-Sha'bi: "I do not believe that Ali ibn Abi Talib used to say: 'The waiting period for a woman whose husband has died is the later of the two terms.'"

He said: "Indeed, believe it as strongly as you have ever believed anything. Ali used to say: 'His saying: **وَأُولاتُ الْأَحْمالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ** — "As for those who are pregnant, their term shall be until they deliver their burden" (65:4) is only regarding the divorced woman.'"

In the same source, Abd al-Razzaq narrated from Ubaydullah ibn Abdillah ibn Utbah that Abu Amr ibn Hafs ibn al-Mughirah went out with Ali to Yemen. He sent to his wife, Fatimah bint Qays, the one divorce that remained of her divorces. He ordered al-Harith ibn Hisham and Abbas ibn Abi Rabi'ah to provide her with maintenance. She found it insufficient, so they said to her: "By Allah, you have no maintenance unless you are pregnant." She came to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and mentioned her affair to him. The Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said to her: "You have no maintenance." She asked his permission to move, and he permitted her.

Marwan sent to her asking about that, and she told him. Marwan said: "I have not heard this tradition except from a woman; we shall take hold of the bond (العصمة) that we found the people upon." Fatimah said: "Between me and you is the Book of Allah. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: **وَلا يَخْرُجْنَ ـ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ** — 'nor shall they leave, unless they commit a manifest indecency' (65:1) until He reached: **لا تَدْرِي لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً** — 'You do not know, maybe Allah will bring about some new matter after that' (65:1)." She said: "This is for the one who has the right of return; so what 'matter' can be brought about after three [divorces]? How can you say: 'There is no maintenance if she is not pregnant'? For what reason then do you confine her?"

But he should leave her until she menstruates and becomes pure, then divorce her with one divorce. If she menstruates, her waiting period is three menstruations. If she does not menstruate, her waiting period is three months. If she is pregnant, her waiting period is that she delivers her burden. If he wishes to take her back before her waiting period concludes, he takes two men as witnesses, as Allah said: **وَأَشْهِدُوا ذَوَيْ عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ** — "and take as witness two honest men from among yourselves" (65:2) at the time of divorce and at the time of return.

If he takes her back, she remains with him with two divorces remaining. If he does not take her back, then when her waiting period concludes, she is irrevocably separated from him by one [divorce] and she has more authority over herself; then she may marry whomever she wishes, him or someone else.
